mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and eases financial conditions, supporting equity multiples and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when the easing trend is sustained.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher than baseline when FEDFUNDS falls for at least 2 consecutive monthly observations, or if triggered windows show negative average returns.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL acceleration above the prior 3-month pace forces a tighter real-rate narrative and raises bond-market discount rates, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above its prior 12-month median
failure_condition: Reject if equity returns are not negative in inflation-acceleration trigger windows, or if the effect disappears after controlling for concurrent FEDFUNDS level changes.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in UNRATE signals weakening household income prospects and softer demand, which should reduce S&P 500 returns over the next month as earnings expectations are revised down.
failure_condition: Reject if rising UNRATE periods fail to produce lower-than-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign turns positive in any sustained labor-weakening window.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST rises sharply, it indicates housing-demand momentum and broader cyclical strength, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter through improved growth expectations and cyclical earnings.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises more than 5% above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if high-HOUST trigger windows do not outperform baseline 60-day returns, or if the average return is negative after a housing surge.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in INDPRO together with stronger PAYEMS indicates synchronized supply-side and labor-market improvement, which should support corporate revenues and margins, delivering positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint INDPRO-PAYEMS expansion signal does not outperform either series alone, or if forward returns are not positive when both are rising together.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in RSAFS captures weakening retail sales and consumer spending, which should translate into lower S&P 500 returns over the next week as near-term revenue expectations deteriorate.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS falls for 2 consecutive monthly readings
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive RSAFS declines are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the signal has no incremental downside versus baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising PAYEMS and stable or falling UNRATE indicates labor-market resilience, which should support risk appetite and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises while UNRATE does not increase over the same monthly observation
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS-up/UNRATE-flat windows do not yield above-baseline 20-day returns, or if the positive effect disappears when UNRATE is also rising.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a high FEDFUNDS level suppresses equity multiples and should generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days, especially when CPI inflation is still elevated.
failure_condition: Reject if high-FEDFUNDS tightening windows do not produce lower 60-day returns than baseline, or if the signal turns positive when inflation remains above its recent trend.
